summary: RFC 9090 defines CBOR tags for object identifiers (OIDs) and serves as the reference for their IANA registration to ensure extensibility and efficiency in the CBOR data format.

blocks.content: RFC 9090: Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR) Tags for Object Identifiers

source_span.quote: # RFC 9090: Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR) Tags for Object Identifiers

blocks.content: This document defines CBOR tags for object identifiers (OIDs) and is the reference document for the IANA registration of the CBOR tags so defined.

source_span.quote:
This document defines CBOR tags for object identifiers (OIDs) and is
   the reference document for the IANA registration of the CBOR tags so
   defined.

blocks.content: The Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR), defined in RFC 8949, is a data format whose design goals include the possibility of extremely small code size, fairly small message size, and extensibility without the need for version negotiation.

source_span.quote:
The Concise Binary Object Representation (CBOR), defined in RFC 8949,
   is a data format whose design goals include the possibility of
   extremely small code size, fairly small message size, and
   extensibility without the need for version negotiation.

blocks.content: This is an Internet Standards Track document.

source_span.quote: This is an Internet Standards Track document.

blocks.content: This document is a product of the Internet Engineering Task Force (IETF). It represents the consensus of the IETF community. It has received public review and has been approved for publication by the Internet Engineering Steering Group (IESG).

source_span.quote:
This document is a product of the Internet Engineering Task Force
   (IETF).  It represents the consensus of the IETF community.  It has
   received public review and has been approved for publication by the
   Internet Engineering Steering Group (IESG).  Further information on
   Internet Standards is available in Section 2 of RFC 7841.
